_note: Culture Tracker — kits, fan moments, merch, viral stories. Update manually.

items.title: Argentina 2026 Home Kit
items.description: The Albiceleste return with the iconic light blue and white stripes. Adidas introduces a subtle gradient texture across the chest, referencing the Andes.

items.title: USA 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Nike designs the US kit as a co-host statement piece — bold red, white, and blue with a stars motif woven into the fabric. The away is a clean navy with electric trim.

items.title: Mexico 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Adidas reinterprets the classic green with a pre-Columbian pattern woven into the chest. Widely considered the best kit of the tournament.

items.title: Morocco 2026 Away Kit
items.description: PUMA's all-gold away kit for Morocco is already a fan favorite — reminiscent of the 2022 semifinal run. High demand expected.

items.title: Japan 2026 Home Kit
items.description: Adidas uses an origami-inspired pattern on Japan's deep navy home kit. Intricate, minimal, highly wearable. Sells out fast globally.

items.title: Official 2026 World Cup Scarf
items.description: FIFA's official tournament scarf — 'USA·Canada·Mexico 2026' in a classic woven design. Available at host city FanFests and FIFA.com.

items.title: Azteca hosts its third World Cup opener
items.description: Estadio Azteca becomes the first stadium to host three World Cup matches — having hosted the 1970 and 1986 finals. The June 11 opener in Mexico City is already an iconic moment before it begins.

items.title: Tifos, flags, and the art of the fan section
items.description: The American Outlaws (USA), La Doce (Argentina), and Ultras groups from Morocco, Brazil, and Japan are expected to bring some of the most elaborate visual displays the US has ever seen at a soccer match.
